findings.summary: Several top-level UI modules still combine orchestration, state, and rendering
evidence: Structural findings continue to flag these files as large, hook-dense modules with mixed responsibilities.
The same files act as coordination hubs for data transforms, async handlers, and JSX composition in one boundary.
findings.suggestion: Split each module into a container + focused feature slices (state orchestration, async services, and presentational sections) while preserving existing public entrypoints.
findings.reasoning: Clear file-level ownership reduces blast radius and makes onboarding and change review substantially easier.

findings.summary: Generation flow handoffs still require adapters across neighboring modules
evidence: Prompt and generation shaping logic is split across utility layers, forcing repeated boundary normalization between form hooks and task builders.
Core generation helpers still expose mixed sync/async and contract styles, creating seam friction in caller modules.
findings.suggestion: Define one boundary policy module for prompt normalization and one for generation payload contracts, then route current call sites through those two seams incrementally.
findings.reasoning: Cleaner handoffs across adjacent modules reduce duplicate glue logic and make failure/debug paths more predictable.
